interface: Cluster Policies API 2.0 (/api/2.0/policies/clusters) + Policy Families API 2.0 (/api/2.0/policy-families)

auth.redaction: the token must never appear in exception text or logs

list.success: 200 with {policies: [Policy...]} — a SINGLE unpaginated document; this endpoint has no page token
list.policy_fields: policy_id, name (1-100 chars, unique), definition (policy-definition JSON as a STRING), description, is_default, libraries, max_clusters_per_user, policy_family_id, policy_family_definition_overrides, created_at_timestamp, creator_user_name

create.body: name plus EITHER definition OR policy_family_id (+ optional policy_family_definition_overrides), optional description/max_clusters_per_user/libraries
create.constraint: definition and policy_family_id are mutually exclusive; family customization goes through policy_family_definition_overrides, whose rules merge into the inherited family definition

edit.body: policy_id plus the same fields as create; fields omitted from the payload are dropped from the policy, so unmanaged settings (libraries, description, max_clusters_per_user) must be carried over from the existing policy

families_list.success: 200 with {policy_families: [{policy_family_id, name, description, definition, version}], next_page_token?} — THIS listing is the paginated one
families_list.note: policy families are Databricks-managed and read-only

semantic_compare.rule: definition and policy_family_definition_overrides are JSON documents serialized as strings; equality is by parsed tree (object key order and whitespace are irrelevant, numbers compare numerically so 8 == 8.0), never by string comparison

default_policies.rule: is_default policies are created and managed by Databricks; the reconciler must never edit them
